zotero.abstractNote: We study the problem of releasing a dataset under differential privacy (DP) such that the released data is useful for a class of Lipschitz functions. We focus on the setting where the functions are sparse, meaning they only depend on a small subset of the features. We provide a new mechanism for this problem that achieves a better utility-privacy trade-off than existing methods. Our mechanism is based on a combination of the exponential mechanism and a new technique for sparse estimation. We also provide a certified privacy guarantee for our mechanism, which means that the privacy loss is bounded even if the data distribution is unknown.
zotero.conferenceName: Theory and Practice of Differential Privacy
zotero.proceedingsTitle: TPDP 2023 - Theory and Practice of Differential Privacy
